documentation: # Backend.AI WSProxy

functions.name: ai.backend.wsproxy.utils.mime_match
documentation:
Checks if `base_array` MIME string contains `compare` MIME type.

:param: base_array: Array of MIME strings to be compared, concatenated with comma (,) delimiter.
:param: compare: MIME string to compare.
:param: strict: If set to True, do not allow wildcard on source MIME type.

documentation: Returns the API version number.

documentation: Start the wsproxy service as a foreground process.

variables.name: ai.backend.wsproxy.proxy.backend.http.CHUNK_SIZE
documentation: 1 KiB

modules.name: ai.backend.wsproxy.exceptions
documentation:
This module defines a series of Backend.AI-specific errors based on HTTP Error
classes from aiohttp.
Raising a BackendError is automatically mapped to a corresponding HTTP error
response with RFC7807-style JSON-encoded description in its response body.

In the client side, you should use "type" field in the body to distinguish
canonical error types beacuse "title" field may change due to localization and
future UX improvements.

classes.name: ai.backend.wsproxy.exceptions.BackendError
documentation:
An RFC-7807 error class as a drop-in replacement of the original
aiohttp.web.HTTPError subclasses.

functions.name: ai.backend.wsproxy.api.conf.conf_v2
documentation:
Generates and returns a token which will be used as an authentication credential for
 /v2/proxy/{token}/{session}/add request.

functions.name: ai.backend.wsproxy.api.utils.mask_sensitive_keys
documentation:
Returns a new cloned mapping by masking the values of
sensitive keys with "***" from the given mapping.

functions.name: ai.backend.wsproxy.api.utils.call_non_bursty
documentation:
Execute a coroutine once upon max_bursts bursty invocations or max_idle
milliseconds after bursts smaller than max_bursts.

ai.backend.wsproxy.api.circuit.delete_circuit
documentation: Removes circuit record from wsproxy.

functions.name: ai.backend.wsproxy.api.endpoint.create_or_update_endpoint
documentation: Creates or updates an inference circuit.

functions.name: ai.backend.wsproxy.api.endpoint.remove_endpoint
documentation: Deassociates inference circuit from system.

functions.name: ai.backend.wsproxy.api.endpoint.generate_endpoint_api_token
documentation:
Creates and returns API token required for execution of model service apps hosted by AppProxy.
 This API is meant to be called from Backend.AI manager rather than model service callee itself.

params.name:
Field(
        description=textwrap.dedent(
            """
                key-value pair of available applications exposed by requested endpoint.
                Key should be name of the app, and value as list of host-port pairs app is bound to.
                """
        ),
    )

Field(
        default=False,
        description=textwrap.dedent(
            """
                If set to true, AppProxy will require an API token (which can be obtained from `generate_endpoint_api_token` request)
                fullfilled at request header.
                """
        ),
    )

class_variables.name: ai.backend.wsproxy.api.endpoint.EndpointAPITokenGenerationRequestModel.user_uuid
documentation: Token requester's user UUID.

class_variables.name: ai.backend.wsproxy.api.endpoint.EndpointAPITokenGenerationRequestModel.exp
documentation: Expiration date of token.

classes.name: ai.backend.wsproxy.types.FrontendMode
documentation: FIXME: merge majority of common definitions to ai.backend.common when ready

params.name:
Field(
        str,
        description="Name of the Backend.AI Kernel app circuit is hosting. Can be a blank string if circuit is referencing an inference app.",
    )
Field(ProxyProtocol, description="Protocol of the Backend.AI Kernel app.")
Field(UUID, description="ID of the worker hosting the circuit.")

Field(
        bool,
        description=textwrap.dedent(
            """
                Shows if the circuit is open to public.
                For interactive apps, this set as true means users without authorization cookie set will also be able to access application.
                For inference apps it means that API will work without authorization token passed.
                """
        ),
    )

Field(
        str | None,
        description="Comma separated list of CIDRs accepted as traffic source. null means the circuit is accessible anywhere.",
    )
Field(str | None, description="Session owner's access key.")
Field(list[RouteInfo], description="List of kernel access information.")

documentation:
Deprecated: only for legacy applications. Just call `proxy` API directly.
Returns URL to AppProxy's `proxy` API handler.
